title: Entada coulteri
intro: Entada coulteri, also known as Coulter's entada or sea bean, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and acting as an antioxidant due to its rich content of bioactive compounds, such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids. The bioactive constituents of Entada coulteri, including coulterine, coulterinol, and other isoflavones, have been shown to exhibit anti-diabetic, anti-hypertensive, and anti-cancer properties. In traditional medicine, Entada coulteri is often prepared as a tea or decoction, as well as being used in capsule or tablet form, to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Entada coulteri is classified in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the pea or legume family. This family is a large and diverse group of plants, often found in tropical and subtropical regions. Within the Fabaceae family, Entada coulteri is further classified as a member of the subfamily Faboideae, which includes many other legume species. The genus Entada itself is a group of climbing legumes native to tropical Asia and Australia. The classification of Entada coulteri reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Fabaceae family.
uses: Entada coulteri is traditionally used in some cultures to treat various ailments due to its potential medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds that may have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. In traditional medicine, it is often used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Some studies suggest that the plant's extracts may have antioxidant and hepatoprotective properties, making it a potential candidate for treating liver-related diseases. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Entada coulteri.
benefits: Entada coulteri is a plant species that has been studied for its potential health benefits, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant is said to have antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infections and promote wound healing.
